rsmTopic: Set theory: defined sets, equal sets, empty sets
description: Understanding sets and their properties - RSM introduces this in grade 3, well above US standards
notes: Set theory is foundational for logic and later algebra. US Common Core introduces this implicitly in grade 4-5.

description: Using Venn diagrams to show relationships between sets
notes: Visual logic tool. Bridges set theory to later probability and statistics.

description: Multiplying numbers like 234 x 6 using standard algorithm
notes: CCSS places 4.NBT.B.5 at grade 4. RSM does this in grade 3.

rsmTopic: Multiplication and division by powers of 10
description: Understanding how multiplying/dividing by 10, 100, 1000 shifts digits
notes: Connects to place value understanding. CCSS 4.NBT.A.1.

rsmTopic: Units of measurement: length, weight, time
description: Converting between measurement units and solving measurement word problems
notes: CCSS 4.MD.A.1. RSM includes more complex conversions.

description: Multiplying numbers like 34 x 56 or 123 x 45
notes: CCSS 4.NBT.B.5 covers 2-digit multiplication. RSM extends to 3-digit in grade 3.

description: Line symmetry, reflection, translation of shapes on a plane
notes: CCSS addresses this in grade 4 (4.G.A.3) and grade 5 geometry.

description: Using letters to represent unknown quantities in simple equations
notes: CCSS introduces expressions in grade 5 (5.OA.A). RSM does this in grade 3 Q2.

description: Solving word problems using the distance formula d = v × t
notes: This is formally a middle school topic (grade 6-7 in US). RSM covers it in grade 3. Requires multi-digit multiplication.

description: Applying multiplication to real-world cost calculations
notes: Practical application of multiplication. Builds formula-thinking habits.

description: Solving problems about work done at a given rate over time
notes: This is typically a grade 6-7 concept in US curriculum. RSM introduces it in grade 3.

description: Solving word problems that require 3 or more operations
notes: CCSS grade 3 covers 2-step problems. RSM grade 3 Q4 covers compound multi-step problems.

description: Finding equivalent fractions and comparing fractions with different denominators
notes: Aligns well with CCSS grade 4 (4.NF.A.1, 4.NF.A.2).

description: Adding and subtracting fractions with like and unlike denominators
notes: CCSS places unlike denominators at grade 5 (5.NF.A.1). RSM covers at grade 4.

description: Reading, writing, and comparing decimal numbers
notes: Aligns with CCSS grade 4 (4.NF.C.6, 4.NF.C.7).

rsmTopic: Area and perimeter of complex figures
description: Computing area and perimeter of L-shapes and composite figures
notes: CCSS covers this at grade 5-6.

description: Plotting points using (x, y) coordinates in the first quadrant
notes: CCSS grade 5 (5.G.A.1).

description: Solving equations of the form x + 5 = 12 or 3x = 18
notes: Extends the variable intro from grade 3.

description: Multiplying and dividing fractions and mixed numbers
notes: CCSS grade 5 (5.NF.B.4, 5.NF.B.7).

description: Understanding ratio notation and solving proportions
notes: CCSS grade 6 (6.RP.A.1-3). RSM introduces at grade 5.

description: Converting between fractions, decimals, and percentages. Percent of a number.
notes: CCSS addresses formally at grade 6. RSM covers at grade 5.

description: Number line with negative numbers, ordering, and absolute value
notes: CCSS grade 6 (6.NS.C.5-7).

description: Solving one-variable linear equations with variables on both sides
notes: CCSS grade 7 (7.EE.B.4). RSM covers at grade 5.

description: Computing and interpreting measures of central tendency
notes: CCSS grade 6 (6.SP.B.5).

description: Systematic counting, fundamental counting principle, tree diagrams
notes: Combinatorics is a RSM specialty. Introduced informally in grade 4, more formally in grade 5-6.
